Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"mention_owners","description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"}}]}}

Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. 2026-03-26T17:26:43.1661473Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-26T17:26:30.061448649Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57509,\"title\":\"Add CI validation for schema-to-binder property parity\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThere is no automated validation to ensure that all properties defined in \\\\ConfigurationSchema.json\\\\ files are handled by the configuration binder, and vice versa. This means schema properties could drift out of sync with the actual implementation — properties could be added to the binder without updating the schema, or schema properties could reference non-existent binder properties.\\n\\n## Proposal\\n\\nAdd CI validation that:\\n1. Ensures all properties defined in the schema have corresponding binder handling\\n2. Ensures all bindable properties are represented in the schema\\n3. Runs as part of the build/CI pipeline for packages that have a \\\\ConfigurationSchema.json\\\\\\n\\nThis would also help answer questions about beta/preview properties — if a property exists in the binder for a given package version, it should exist in the schema for that version.\\n\\n## Parent\\n\\n#55538\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57509\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"m-nash\",\"id\":64171366,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/m-nash\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/64171366?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Client\",\"SDK Dev Experience\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-26T17:23:34Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-26T17:23:34Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

This means schema properties could drift out of sync with the actual implementation — properties could be added to the binder without updating the schema, or schema properties could reference non-existent binder properties.\\n\\n## Proposal\\n\\nAdd CI validation that:\\n1. Ensures all properties defined in the schema have corresponding binder handling\\n2. Ensures all bindable properties are represented in the schema\\n3. 2026-03-26T17:26:43.2122819Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-03-26T17:26:43.2123091Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-03-26T17:26:43.2123248Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-03-26T17:26:43.2123468Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
2026-03-26T17:26:43.2123807Z [36;1m# Only run awf logs summary if awf command exists (it may not be installed if workflow failed before install step)[0m
2026-03-26T17:26:43.2123920Z [36;1mif command -v awf &> /dev/null; then[0m
